message.content:
{
  "homework": {
    "sessionInformation": {
      "sessionNumber": "1",
      "sessionTitle": "Chemical Substances and Their Properties",
      "subject": "Science (086)",
      "grade": "Class X / 10 / General",
      "difficultyLevel": "Balanced",
      "learningPace": "Balanced",
      "estimatedHomeworkDuration": "45 minutes"
    },
    "homework": [
      {
        "id": 1,
        "type": "MCQs",
        "title": "Identifying Chemical Properties",
        "learningOutcomeIds": ["LO_01"],
        "topicCoverage": ["Chemical Substances", "Physical Properties", "Chemical Properties"],
        "difficulty": "Medium",
        "marks": 4,
        "estimatedTime": "8 minutes",
        "instructions": "Select the correct option for each question based on the properties of chemical substances discussed in class.",
        "question": [
          {
            "q_text": "Which of the following is a physical property?",
            "options": ["Reactivity with acid", "Melting point", "Combustibility", "Toxicity"],
            "answerSpace": "Write the correct option letter."
          },
          {
            "q_text": "Which substance turns blue litmus paper red?",
            "options": ["Water", "Acid", "Base", "Salt solution"],
            "answerSpace": "Write the correct option letter."
          },
          {
            "q_text": "The reaction of a metal with oxygen to form a metal oxide is an example of:",
            "options": ["Physical change", "Chemical change", "Reversible change", "Periodic change"],
            "answerSpace": "Write the correct option letter."
          },
          {
            "q_text": "Which property allows a substance to be drawn into wires?",
            "options": ["Malleability", "Ductility", "Conductivity", "Luster"],
            "answerSpace": "Write the correct option letter."
          }
        ],
        "visualRequirement": "None",
        "expectedResponse": "1. b, 2. b, 3. b, 4. b"
      },
      {
        "id": 2,
        "type": "Short Answer",
        "title": "Differentiating Physical and Chemical Changes",
        "learningOutcomeIds": ["LO_01", "LO_02"],
        "topicCoverage": ["Physical Change", "Chemical Change", "Properties of Matter"],
        "difficulty": "Medium",
        "marks": 6,
        "estimatedTime": "12 minutes",
        "instructions": "Define physical and chemical changes. Provide two examples for each from your daily life.",
        "question": "Explain the difference between a physical change and a chemical change with examples.",
        "options": [],
        "answerSpace": "Write your answer in 3-4 sentences per example.",
        "visualRequirement": "None",
        "expectedResponse": "Physical change: No new substance is formed (e.g., melting ice). Chemical change: New substances are formed with different properties (e.g., rusting iron)."
      },
      {
        "id": 3,
        "type": "Diagram Labelling",
        "title": "Observation to Conclusion",
        "learningOutcomeIds": ["LO_02", "LO_03"],
        "topicCoverage": ["Experimental Observation", "Chemical Reactions"],
        "difficulty": "High",
        "marks": 5,
        "estimatedTime": "10 minutes",
        "instructions": "Draw a labelled diagram of the reaction between magnesium ribbon and oxygen. Describe the observation and write the balanced chemical equation.",
        "question": "Describe the experiment where magnesium burns in air. Draw the setup and write the equation.",
        "options": [],
        "answerSpace": "Include a sketch of the burning magnesium and the resulting ash.",
        "visualRequirement": "Draw a labelled diagram showing the burning magnesium ribbon, the flame, and the white ash (Magnesium Oxide).",
        "expectedResponse": "Observation: Bright white light and white powder. Equation: 2Mg + O2 -> 2MgO"
      },
      {
        "id": 4,
        "type": "Real-life Application",
        "title": "Corrosion and Prevention",
        "learningOutcomeIds": ["LO_03"],
        "topicCoverage": ["Chemical Properties", "Real-world Applications", "Corrosion"],
        "difficulty": "Medium",
        "marks": 4,
        "estimatedTime": "8 minutes",
        "instructions": "Identify a common chemical property of iron that causes damage to vehicles. Suggest two methods to prevent it.",
        "question": "Why do iron gates rust? Explain the chemical process and suggest two ways to stop it.",
        "options": [],
        "answerSpace": "Write a short paragraph explaining the cause and prevention.",
        "visualRequirement": "None",
        "expectedResponse": "Cause: Reaction with oxygen and moisture (oxidation). Prevention: Painting, greasing, galvanization."
      },
      {
        "id": 5,
        "type": "Home Experiment",
        "title": "Observing Chemical Changes at Home",
        "learningOutcomeIds": ["LO_02"],
        "topicCoverage": ["Chemical Reactions", "Observation Skills"],
        "difficulty": "Low",
        "marks": 3,
        "estimatedTime": "15 minutes",
        "instructions": "Perform a safe home activity to observe a chemical change. Record your observations.",
        "question": "Mix vinegar and baking soda in a small bowl. Observe the reaction and write down what you see.",
        "options": [],
        "answerSpace": "Record the bubbles, sound, or temperature change observed.",
        "visualRequirement": "None (Observation based)",
        "expectedResponse": "Bubbles of gas (CO2) are released, indicating a chemical change."
      }
    ],
    "summary": {
      "totalQuestions": 5,
      "totalMarks": 22,
      "estimatedCompletionTime": "45 minutes",
      "learningOutcomesCovered": ["LO_01", "LO_02", "LO_03"],
      "topicsCovered": ["Chemical Substances", "Physical Properties", "Chemical Properties", "Physical Change", "Chemical Change", "Experimental Observation", "Real-world Applications", "Corrosion"],
      "subtopicsCovered": ["Reactivity", "Litmus Test", "Metal Oxides", "Ductility", "Rusting", "Prevention Methods"],
      "taskDistribution": {
        "MCQs": 1,
        "Short Answer": 1,
        "Diagram Labelling": 1,
        "Real-life Application": 1,
        "Home Experiment": 1
      },
      "homeExperimentIncluded": true,
      "parentEngagementIncluded": false
    }
  }
}
